en.extract:
The South Moluccan cicadabird (Edolisoma amboinense) is a passerine bird in the family Campephagidae found on the islands of Buru, Ambon, and Seram in the Maluku Islands of Indonesia. The species was formerly considered conspecific with the common cicadabird, now renamed the Sahul cicadabird.


Taxonomy
The South Moluccan cicadabird was formally described in 1865 by German ornithologist Gustav Hartlaub based on a specimen collected on Ambon Island (Ambon) in the Maluku Islands. He coined the binomial name Ceblepyris amboinense. The South Moluccan cicadabird was formerly treated as conspecific with the common cicadabird (now renamed the Sahul cicadabird) (Edolisoma tenuirostre) but has since been elevated to species status. The species is monotypic: no subspecies are recognised.

The '''South Moluccan cicadabird''' ('''''Edolisoma amboinense''''') is a [[passerine]] bird in the family [[Campephagidae]] found on the islands of [[Buru]], [[Ambon Island|Ambon]], and [[Seram Island|Seram]] in the [[Maluku Islands]] of Indonesia. The species was formerly considered [[conspecific]] with the common cicadabird, now renamed the [[Sahul cicadabird]].

==Taxonomy==
The South Moluccan cicadabird was [[species description|formally described]] in 1865 by German ornithologist [[Gustav Hartlaub]] based on a specimen collected on Ambon Island ([[Ambon Island|Ambon]]) in the [[Maluku Islands]]. He coined the [[binomial name]] ''Ceblepyris amboinense''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Hartlaub | first=Gustav | author-link=Gustav Hartlaub | date=1865 | title=Monographische Studien über die Gruppe der Campephaginen | journal=Journal für Ornithologie | language=Latin, German | volume=13 | issue=75 | pages=153–173 [156] | doi=10.1007/BF02317698 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/35463226 }}</ref> The South Moluccan cicadabird was formerly treated as [[conspecific]] with the common cicadabird (now renamed the [[Sahul cicadabird]]) (''Edolisoma tenuirostre'') but has since been elevated to species status.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Bristlehead, butcherbirds, woodswallows, Mottled Berryhunter, ioras, cuckooshrikes | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/butcherbirds/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=25 September 2024 }}</ref> The species is [[monotypic]]: no [[subspecies]] are recognised.<ref name=ioc/>
